rulings.Case Title: ROBERT KACSUTA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Schneider Electric USA¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to November 8 per the October 25 stipulated order. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Mr. Cooks¿ deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. McLean¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Pantaleoni¿s deposition testimony and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Gottschall was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cooks¿ declaration are overruled, except to the extent that his declaration references at least ten occasions of working with new Crane Co. valves, which are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. McLean¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Pantaleoni¿s deposition testimony and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
